Since logx is concave, we can implies HP,Q​(X)−HP​(X)​=−xk​∈X∑​P(xk​)log(P(xk​)Q(xk​)​)=Ep​[−log(P(xk​)Q(xk​)​)]​ Let T=P(x)Q(x)​, then Ep​[−logT]≥−log[Ep​T]=−log[k∑​P(xk​)P(xk​)Q(k)​]=0 The equality holds only when P=Q.

Kullback-Leibler divergence (KL-divergence)

The distance between two distributions.

DKL​(P∥Q)=HP,Q​(X)−HP​(X)=∫x∈X​pX​(x)log(qX​(x)pX​(x)​) dx

Property 1 DKL​(P∥Q)≥0

Proof DKL​(P∥Q)​=−∫x∈X​pX​(x)log(pX​(x)qX​(x)​) dx≥−∫x∈X​pX​(x)(pX​(x)qX​(x)​−1) dx=−∫x∈X​pX​(x)−qX​(x) dx=∫x∈X​qX​(x) dx−∫x∈X​pX​(x) dx=0​

Property 2 DKL​(P∥Q)=DKL​(Q∥P)

Linear Regression

Modeling of Linear Regression

Deterministic Perspective

Find w which minimizes the following expression:

m1​i=1∑m​(fw​(xi​)−yi​)2

Probabilistic Perspective

y=w⊤x+e, where e∼N(0,σ2)

where e is called obervation noise or residual error. The output y can also be seen as a random variable, whose conditional probability is :

p(y∣x,w)=N(w⊤x,σ2)

Given the training dataset D={(xi​,yi​)}i=1m​, by maximum log-likehood estimaiton, we get

logL(w;D)​=log(i=1∏m​p(yi​∣xi​,w))=i=1∑m​logN(w⊤x,σ2)=i=1∏n​log(2πσ2​1​exp(−2σ2(yi​−w⊤xi​)2​))=−mlog(σ(2π)21​)−2σ21​i=1∑m​(yi​−w⊤xi​)2​

wMLE​=argmaxlogL(w;D)=wargmin​21​i=1∑m​(yi​−w⊤xi​)2

Solutions of Linear Regression

Analytical solution

Let e=Xw−y, then

J(w)​=i=1∑m​(fw​(xi​)−yi​)2=e⊤e=w⊤X⊤Xw−2y⊤Xw+y⊤y​

Differentiating J(w) with respect to w and setting the result to 0:

∂w∂​​=0(X⊤X is symmetric)⟹w⊤X⊤Xw=y⊤Xw​

If X⊤X is invertible then w=(X⊤X)−1X⊤y

For linear regression with multiple outputs, E=XW−Y, then

J(W)=trace(E⊤E)

Gradient Descent

J(w)=21​i=1∑m​(yi​−w⊤xi​)2=21​∥Xw−y∥2, w can be updated by gradient descent algorithm:

w←w−α∂w∂J(w)​=w−αX⊤(Xw−y)

Classification

Binary Classification

If X⊤X is invertible then w=(X⊤X)−1X⊤y, fw​(xnew​)=sgn(xnew⊤​w).

Multi-Category Classification

Each ro in Y has a one-hot assignment, then If X⊤X is invertible then w=(X⊤X)−1X⊤Y, fw​(xnew​)=i=1,…,Cargmax​(xnew⊤​W).

Linear Regression Models

Ridge Regression

J(w)=i=1∑m​(yi​−xi⊤​w)2+λw⊤w

where w=[0,w1​,w2​,⋯,wd​]⊤ (The bias w0​ should not be normalized).

To get wmin​(Xw−y)⊤(Xw−y)+λw⊤w,

∂w∂​(Xw−y)⊤(Xw−y)+λw⊤w=0⟹w=(X⊤X+λId​^​)−1X⊤y(λ>0)

We can assume that the parameter w follow a zero-mean Gaussian prior p(w)=N(w∣0,τ2I) (omit the bias w0​).

Utilizing this prior, we obtain the maximum a posteriori (MAP) estimation

wMAP​​=p(w∣x,y)=wargmax​[i=1∑m​logp(yi​∣xi​,w)+logp(w)]=wargmax​[C−2σ21​i=1∑m​(yi​−xi⊤​w)2−2τ21​∥w∥2]≡wargmin​[i=1∑m​(yi​−xi⊤​w)2−λ∥w∥2](λ=τ2σ2​)​

Polynomial Regression

ϕ(x)=[1,x1​,…,xd​,…,xi​xj​,…,xi​xj​xk​,…]⊤w=[w0​,w1​,…,wd​,…,wi,j​,…,wi,j,k​,…]⊤fw​(x)=ϕ(x)⊤w

Note that fw​(x) is still a linear function w.r.t. w.

Lasso Regression

p(w)=Lap(w∣0,λ)=21​exp(−λ∥w∥1​​), then we have wMAP​≡wargmin​[i=1∑m​(yi​−xi⊤​w)2−α∥w∥1​].

Robust Linear Regression

Adpot the l1​ loss as follows J(w)=i=1∑m​∣xi⊤​w−yi​∣.

Assuming that p(y∣x,w,b)=(y∣w⊤x,b)∝exp(−b1​∣y−w⊤x∣), wMLE​≡wargmin​b1​i=1∑m​∣xi⊤​w−yi​∣.

Two ways to make it differentiable:

  • w,tmin​i∑m​ti​s.t. −ti​≤xi⊤​w−yi​≤ti​,1≤i≤m.

  • By using the following equation ∣a∣=μ>0min​21​(μa2​+μ).

    Then it can be reformulated as follows:

    wmin​μ1​,…,μm​>0min​21​i=1∑m​(μi​(xi⊤​w−yi​)2​+μi​)

    Given w, μi​=∣xi⊤​w−yi​∣; Given μ, w=wargmin​i=1∑m​21​μi​(xi⊤​w−yi​)2​.

Logistic Regression

Hypothesis Function

fw​(x)=g(w⊤x)∈[0,1]g(z)=1+exp(−z)1​g′(z)=g(z)(1−g(z))

Cost Function

  • Linear Regression

    J(w)=2m1​i=1∑m​(fw​(xi​)−yi​)2=2m1​∥Xw−y∥2, H=∇w2​J(w)=m1​X⊤X⪰0⟹convex

  • Logistic Regression

    J(w)=2m1​i=1∑m​(g(w⊤xi​)−yi​)2⟹non-convex

  • Cross-entropy Loss

    cost(y(x),fw​(x))​=−P(y=1∣x)⋅logP(y=1∣x;w)−P(y=0∣x)⋅P(y=0∣x;w)={−log(fw​(x))y(x)=1−log(1−fw​(x))y(x)=0​​

    J(w)=m1​i=1∑m​cost(yi​,fw​(xi​))

    For yi​=1,Ji​(w)=−log(σi​), ∇w​Ji​=−(1−σi​)xi​,∇w2​Ji​=σi​(1−σi​)xi​xi⊤​.

    For yi​=0,Ji​(w)=−log(1−σi​), ∇w​Ji​=σi​⋅xi​,∇w2​Ji​=σi​(1−σi​)xi​xi⊤​.

    ⟹J(w) convex

Multi-class Classification

Softmax

fW(j)​(x)=c=1∑C​exp(wc⊤​x)expwj⊤​x​=P(y=j∣x,W)

∂wj​∂fwc​​​={fwj​​(1−fwj​​)xi​c=j−fwj​​fwc​​xi​c=j​

Cost Function

J(W)=−m1​i∑m​j∑C​[I(yi​=j)log(fwj​​(xi​))]

∂wj​∂J(W)​​=−m1​i∑m​​fwj​​(xi​)I(yi​=j)​⋅∇wj​∇fwj​​(xi​)​+c=j∑C​fwc​​(xi​)I(yi​=c)​⋅∇wj​∇fwc​​(xi​)​​⟹m1​i∑m​(fwj​​(xi​)−I(yi​=j))xi​​

Regularized Logistic Regression

J(w)=J(w)+2mλ​i=1∑d​wj2​=−m1​i∑m​[yi​logfw​(xi​)+(1−yi​)log(1−fw​xi​)]+i=1∑d​wj2​

w0​←w0​−α∂wj​∂J(w)​=wj​−mα​∑i=1m​(fw​(xi​)−yi​)xi​(0)where xi​=1wj​←wj​−α∂wj​∂J(w)​=wj​−mα​∑i=1m​(fw​(xi​)−yi​)xi​(j)+λwj​
